## TD3 Training Pipeline

### Configuration-Driven Experiment Design

Each experiment is fully specified by an external configuration file (YAML
with dotlist override support). Data paths, feature subsets, reward type,
environment settings, and optimization hyperparameters are all externalized.
No source-code changes are required to switch between experimental scenarios.
A pre-training validation stage checks path availability, feature definition
validity, split feasibility, and column compatibility before any computation
begins, preventing wasted runs from misconfigured experiments.

### Environment Construction

After data preparation, the environment is constructed from the processed
training dataset. It exposes a continuous action space $a_t \in [-1, 1]$
representing target portfolio exposure and returns observations composed of
the engineered feature vector defined in Chapter 5. Transaction fees and
position-change costs are applied within the environment step function so
that they are reflected in the reward signal during training.

### Training Loop

Training follows the standard off-policy TD3 loop. At each step the agent
observes state $s_t$, computes action $\mu_\theta(s_t)$, adds exploration
noise $\epsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma)$, and executes the clipped
result. The transition $(s_t, a_t, r_t, s_{t+1})$ is stored in a replay
buffer. Mini-batches sampled from the buffer are used to update the twin
critics against the minimum-target Bellman backup and then to update the
actor via the deterministic policy gradient. The actor and target networks
are updated every $d$ critic steps (delayed update), and target parameters
track main parameters through soft updates with coefficient $\tau$.

Exploration noise is decayed over training to shift the agent from
exploration toward exploitation as the policy matures.

### Checkpointing and Reproducibility

Random seeds are applied consistently across NumPy, PyTorch, and the RL
framework so that runs under identical configurations produce comparable
training trajectories. Checkpoints save model parameters, optimizer states,
and step counters at regular intervals, supporting both interruption recovery
and continuation experiments. Run parameters, metrics, and artifacts are
logged through MLflow, enabling auditable comparison across feature sets,
reward functions, and hyperparameter settings.

### Evaluation Protocol

Final performance is reported on the held-out test split using a separate
evaluation environment built from test data. The evaluation rollout produces
two output streams that are kept strictly separate: the optimization reward
(used as a training diagnostic) and the realized portfolio return series
(used for financial metrics). Conflating these streams — for example,
computing Sharpe ratio from the reward signal rather than from log returns —
invalidates risk-adjusted metrics.

Financial performance metrics computed from the return series include
annualized return, annualized volatility, Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio,
maximum drawdown, win rate, and profit factor. Behavioral outputs include
action trajectories and turnover statistics. All financial metrics are
computed on the same price series as the evaluation rollout to ensure
benchmark comparisons are consistent.
